title: Ageratina dendroides
intro: Ageratina dendroides, also known as tree ageratina or tree snakeroot, is a perennial herb belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina dendroides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ageratina dendroides is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in infusions and capsules, to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Ageratina dendroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Central America, specifically in countries such as Mexico and Costa Rica. This plant is commonly known as the "tree snakeweed" due to its shrub-like growth. Ageratina dendroides is a member of the genus Ageratina, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the genus Eupatorium. The classification of Ageratina dendroides is further divided into different ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.

side_effects: Ageratina dendroides, also known as tree ageratina or false dragon tree, is a plant used in traditional medicine.
